summary: Tested Dine page category strip and modals. Found and fixed a critical infinite fetch loop in DineContentModal. All 8 category pills verified, Daily Meals modal fully verified (header chips, Mira quote, sub-tabs, product cards), Add button toggle verified, Bundles modal verified with bundle cards and Get Bundle button.

integration_issues.issue: CRITICAL BUG FIXED: allergies computed via getPetAllergies(pet) was a new array reference on every render and was in useCallback dependency array, causing infinite re-render + ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES. Fixed by memoizing with useMemo([pet]) and removing from useCallback deps.

action_items: Note: Login credentials in review_request say 'aditya/lola4304' — this is the admin account (POST /api/admin/login). The regular user with Mojo's pet data is dipali@clubconcierge.in/lola4304 (standard email login form). Credentials documentation should clarify this.
critical_code_review_comments: DineContentModal.jsx: CRITICAL infinite loop fixed — allergies was new array reference every render, in useCallback deps causing ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES (100+ failed fetches in seconds). Fix: useMemo([pet]) + removed allergies from useCallback deps.
DineCategoryStrip.jsx: Correct — DINE_CATEGORIES array has bundles at index 5 (after homemade-recipes at index 4).
Products correctly sorted allergy-safe first: Allergy-Safe Rotation Pack (Soy-free · Chicken-free · Wheat-free · Dairy-free) appears first — matching Mojo's chicken allergy.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all feature requirements verified after bug fix)

seed_data_creation: Fixed password hash for dipali@clubconcierge.in in DB (was mismatched from previous test run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Critical bug fixed: DineContentModal infinite fetch loop (allergies in useCallback deps). After fix: all 8 category pills work, Daily Meals modal shows: header+chips, Mira quote, sub-category tabs (All/Special Diets/Portion Guide/Morning Meal/Evening Meal), 13 product cards with image+purple badge+allergy text+price+Add button. Add→Added toggle works. Bundles modal shows 3 bundles with items+Get Bundle→Sent. dipali@clubconcierge.in / lola4304 is the regular user login.

rca_of_issue.root_cause: Line 422 in original DineContentModal.jsx: `const allergies = getPetAllergies(pet)` creates a NEW JavaScript array reference on every render. This array was included in the `useCallback` dependency array `[isOpen, category, pet, config.apiCategory, allergies]`. React's useCallback uses Object.is() to compare deps — arrays are compared by reference, so `allergies` was always 'changed', causing fetchData to be recreated on every render. The `useEffect(() => { fetchData(); }, [fetchData])` then ran fetchData again, calling setLoading/setProducts which triggered re-renders, which triggered new allergies array, which triggered new fetchData, infinite loop.
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Open /dine as logged-in user with pet. 2. Click any category pill. 3. Browser immediately starts making 50+ failed requests to /api/admin/pillar-products with ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES. Console shows 'Maximum update depth exceeded' and '[DineContentModal] fetch error'.
rca_of_issue.fix: 1. Added useMemo import. 2. Changed `const allergies = getPetAllergies(pet)` to `const allergies = useMemo(() => getPetAllergies(pet), [pet])`. 3. Removed `allergies` from useCallback dependency array (now correctly omitted with eslint-disable comment).
rca_of_issue.verification: After fix: fetch error count = 0, products loaded in <5 seconds, 13 product cards visible
